distance_method: driving miles + traffic-aware drive time via Google Routes API (next Sat 11am local departure).

events.title: Jump Flow LA Meetup
events.blurb: Jump rope + double dutch + rope flow + flow arts. All skill levels.
events.description: Free, all-ages community gathering in DTLA where participants jump, do rope flow, and double-dutch at Grand Park. Hosted collaboratively by SLUSHROPES, Let's Jump LA, Jump Flow LA, and Melanated Jump Squad — newcomers and seasoned practitioners both welcome.

events.title: Skirball Puppet Festival
events.blurb: Puppets in all shapes and sizes. Live music + DIY make-your-own-puppet workshop.
events.description: Premier puppetry performances alongside interactive activities — puppet-making workshops, live music, roaming character encounters, pop-up micro shows. Featured image: a child staring excitedly at a red dragon puppet in the courtyard.

events.title: Pups & Putts
events.blurb: 18-hole mini golf alongside adoptable shelter dogs from spcaLA.
events.description: spcaLA hosts an 18-hole mini-golf course on the lawn at Ivy Station in Culver City. Play a round, meet adoptable dogs from the spcaLA Pet Adoption Center, possibly leave with a new best friend.

events.title: CicLAvia: West LA
events.blurb: Several miles of streets closed to cars. Walk, skate, run, or cycle.
events.description: Sunday's car-free open-streets event with the route running from Westwood to Santa Monica. CicLAvia 'catalyzes vibrant public spaces, active transportation and good health' — activity hubs along the way for resting, snacking, and people-watching.

events.title: City of STEM Festival + LA Maker Faire
events.blurb: 200+ exhibitors, hands-on activities, live-action roleplay, Rubik's Cube comp, mini RC lowriders.
events.description: Returns 4/26 to Expo Park with 200+ exhibitors celebrating science, technology, engineering, and math. Billed as 'SoCal's largest celebration of science, making, creativity, and invention' — hands-on activities, food trucks, live entertainment, free.

events.title: Jackalope Indie Artisan Fair
events.blurb: 200+ local indie artisans selling handmade goods. Live music, food vendors, family activities.
events.description: The Jackalope Art Fair returns to Old Pasadena's Central Park 4/25-26 with 200+ local hand-selected artisans offering original fashion, jewelry, home décor, art, and more. Live music, family activities, food vendors throughout the weekend.

events.title: "Spring Into Summer" Pop-Up Choir (Lizzy McAlpine songs)
events.blurb: Show up, sing communally with strangers, leave.
events.description: Fan-organized pop-up choir singing through Lizzy McAlpine's catalog. Low-commitment communal singing with strangers — no audition, no rehearsal, just a one-night thing.

events.title: Mid East Eats
events.blurb: Palestinian home-cook pop-up: musakhan, falafel nachos, kunafeh.
events.description: South-LA Palestinian home cook takeover at Maydan Market: musakhan, falafel nachos, kunafeh. Walk-ins only. NOTE: per @mid.east.eats Instagram the residency runs through 4/26 but recent reporting suggests the final weekend was 4/19-20 — verify before going.

events.title: "Fight Club: A Second Discussion" book club
events.blurb: Yes, that book.
events.description: Book club discussing Chuck Palahniuk's Fight Club, hosted at Peak Design's Venice flagship store. Format unconfirmed via search engines — likely an indie/word-of-mouth gathering organized by the store.

events.title: Gospel Skate LA
events.blurb: Faith-based roller skating jam (currently sold out — waitlist worth a shot).
events.description: Faith-based roller-skating jam at LA Kings Burbank Sports Center. Currently sold out per source — joining the waitlist may still be worth it. Format borrowed from the longer-running Gospel Skate Dallas.

events.title: Theremin: The Very First Electronic Instrument
events.blurb: Live demo + history of Leon Theremin's 1928 invention. Try playing it yourself.
events.description: Hands-on presentation about the theremin — the first mass-produced electronic instrument, played without touching it (just waving hands near antennas). Attendees get to actually play one. Hosted in a small Cypress Park space; the kind of niche history-of-tech meetup you'd never find unless you went looking.

events.title: Night at the Library: A Century of Light
events.venue: LA Central Library, 630 W 5th St, DTLA
events.blurb: Immersive after-hours fest celebrating the library's 100th — 200+ performers, art, food, dancing.
events.description: Once-a-century party at the Richard J. Riordan Central Library: 200+ performers, artists, and storytellers wander the stacks alongside light installations, interactive art, live music, and dancing. The library you know during the day, transformed.

events.title: Renaissance Pleasure Faire
events.venue: Santa Fe Dam Rec Area, 15501 E Arrow Hwy, Irwindale
events.blurb: 20-acre Elizabethan world: full-armor jousts, period acts, costume welcome.
events.description: Weekends Apr 4 – May 17. Twenty acres of Elizabethan re-enactment in Irwindale: armored jousting tournaments, period stage acts, themed weekends, tea with the queen. Costumes encouraged but not required. Tickets ~$53 adult.

events.title: May The Force Seduce You — Burlesque Parody
events.venue: The Cat's Crawl, 660 Heliotrope Dr, East Hollywood
events.blurb: Star Wars burlesque parody. Yes, that's exactly what it is.
events.description: Comedic burlesque parody at the Cat's Crawl, a Heliotrope-district performance hall (also a private-event/film space). The May 4 Star Wars Day energy applied to a burlesque revue. Tickets from $62. Two-night run May 1-2.

events.title: May the 4th Be With You — LA Underground Rave
events.venue: Catch One, 4067 W Pico Blvd, Mid-City
events.blurb: Star Wars-themed underground rave. Cosmic décor, lasers, two stages, costumes welcome.
events.description: Underground rave at Catch One built around the May 4 / Star Wars Day theme: cosmic décor, laser shows, two stages of EDM, space-inspired costumes encouraged, photo booth. Doors 8:30pm. Free entry per the Eventbrite listing.

events.title: A Current Affair: Pop-Up Vintage Marketplace
events.blurb: 50+ premier vintage retailers — Victorian to present. Curated.
events.description: Twice-yearly curated vintage shopping: 50+ premier dealers showing clothing, accessories, and jewelry from the Victorian era through present day. Saturday early-admission gets you first pick. Spring 2026 edition.

events.title: It's UNCOMMON LOVE: Spring Tea Gathering
events.venue: Barbara Morrison Performing Arts Ctr, 4305 Degnan Blvd, Leimert Park
events.blurb: Charity tea + fashion + art benefiting women's education programs.
events.description: Springtime fundraiser at the Barbara Morrison Performing Arts Center in Leimert Park: tea service, a fashion presentation, and art exhibits, with proceeds supporting education and entrepreneurship training for women and underserved communities. Listed as 'almost full' — book ahead.

events.title: Artisan Ravioli Making Class
events.venue: Green Haus Kitchen Studio, 7416 Vermont Ave, South LA
events.blurb: Hands-on: dough, sheet rolling, ricotta filling, romesco sauce.
events.description: Three-ish hour pasta workshop: mixing dough, rolling sheets, prepping ricotta filling, building a romesco sauce. You leave knowing how to make ravioli from scratch. Recurring; Sunday 5/3 is one of the dates.

events.title: Smorgasburg LA — AAPI Month Sunday
events.blurb: 5-acre weekly food + vintage market. May Sundays spotlight AAPI vendors.
events.description: Every Sunday at ROW DTLA: dozens of food vendors plus design, vintage, and craft stalls across a five-acre open-air site. Throughout May, Smorgasburg spotlights AAPI bakers, chefs, and cookbook authors in honor of AAPI Heritage Month. Free entry, giant parking garage.

events.title: LA Immersive Invitational 2026
events.venue: After Hours Theatre Company, Los Angeles
events.blurb: Four immersive pieces built in 48 hours by LA's top immersive storytellers. Competition format.
events.description: Fifth-annual showcase from the Immersive Experience Institute: four teams of LA immersive-theatre artists get less than 48 hours to build, rehearse, and stage new pieces. Audience journeys through all four — part competition, part incubator. Sunday 5/3 only.

events.title: Sunset Vibes Silent Disco at Venice Beach
events.blurb: 5 DJs on 5 channels, wireless headphones, beachfront.
events.description: Recurring silent disco at Venice's Waterfront: pick one of five DJ channels through wireless LED headphones, dance on the beach at sunset. Dining on-site. Runs weekly — check Fever for the current Saturday slot.

events.title: It's My Friday Every Sunday — Strange Hotels at Davey Wayne's
events.venue: Good Times at Davey Wayne's, 1611 N El Centro Ave, Hollywood
events.blurb: Weekly 70s-themed Sunday party inside a walk-through-refrigerator speakeasy.
events.description: Recurring Sunday-night party at the 70s-themed Hollywood speakeasy (entrance is through a vintage refrigerator in a fake living room). Indie-electro duo Strange Hotels performs live, DJ spins 70s deep cuts, charismatic host emceeing. Dress code: California casual, no athletic wear after dark.

events.title: TEDx Pasadena 2026 — Growth Through Resilience
events.venue: Boston Court Pasadena, 70 N Mentor Ave
events.blurb: Day of TEDx talks spanning tech, creativity, and human health.
events.description: Annual TEDx day in Pasadena — this year's theme is 'Growth Through Resilience.' Speaker lineup includes EJ Egghart, Elizabeth Yang, Kevin Kay, Edgar McGregor, Brandon Kazen-Maddox, and others pushing the edges of their fields. Tickets go fast.

events.title: Black Writers Weekend — LA Pop-Up Experience
events.venue: Cal State LA, 1994 Paseo Rancho Castilla
events.blurb: Dear Black Writers screening + artist panel + creative mixer at CSULA.
events.description: Los Angeles stop of the touring Black Writers Weekend by AAMBC. Intimate creative gathering: exclusive screening of Dear Black Writers, artist panel with creatives from books/film/media, and a curated networking mixer. One of several city pop-ups in 2026.

events.title: Renaissance Pleasure Faire
events.venue: Santa Fe Dam Rec Area, 15501 E Arrow Hwy, Irwindale
events.blurb: 20-acre Elizabethan world: full-armor jousts, period acts, costume welcome.
events.description: Penultimate weekend of the 2026 run (ends 5/17). Twenty acres of Elizabethan re-enactment in Irwindale: armored jousting tournaments, period stage acts, themed weekends, tea with the queen. Costumes encouraged but not required.

events.title: Rose Bowl Flea Market
events.blurb: Monthly 2,500-vendor flea — rusty signs, 70s designer coats, 20k shoppers.
events.description: Every second Sunday, 2,500+ vendors set up shop across the Rose Bowl parking acreage and 20,000+ shoppers show up. The self-described 'world's most unusual flea market.' VIP ($20) gets you early-bird 5-8:45am; regular admission ($13) 9am-3pm. Kids under 12 free with adult.

events.title: Smorgasburg LA — AAPI Month Sunday
events.blurb: 5-acre weekly food + vintage market. May Sundays spotlight AAPI vendors.
events.description: Every Sunday at ROW DTLA: dozens of food vendors plus design, vintage, and craft stalls across a five-acre open-air site. Throughout May, Smorgasburg spotlights AAPI bakers, chefs, and cookbook authors in honor of AAPI Heritage Month. Free entry, giant parking garage.

events.title: LA Game Con — Edu 2026
events.venue: LA Trade Technical College, 400 W Washington Blvd
events.blurb: Full-day convention for gaming + education — workshops, game-based learning, tournaments.
events.description: All-day gaming-and-education convention at LA Trade Tech: workshops, networking with educators and game-design folks, sessions on game-based learning trends. 12 hours of programming.

events.title: Sunset Vibes Silent Disco at Venice Beach
events.blurb: 5 DJs on 5 channels, wireless headphones, beachfront.
events.description: Recurring silent disco at Venice's Waterfront: pick one of five DJ channels through wireless LED headphones, dance on the beach at sunset. Dining on-site. Runs weekly — check Fever for the current Saturday slot.

events.title: It's My Friday Every Sunday — Strange Hotels at Davey Wayne's
events.venue: Good Times at Davey Wayne's, 1611 N El Centro Ave, Hollywood
events.blurb: Weekly 70s-themed Sunday party inside a walk-through-refrigerator speakeasy.
events.description: Recurring Sunday-night party at the 70s-themed Hollywood speakeasy (entrance is through a vintage refrigerator in a fake living room). Indie-electro duo Strange Hotels performs live, DJ spins 70s deep cuts, charismatic host emceeing. Dress code: California casual, no athletic wear after dark.

events.title: Story Jam — storytelling meets improv
events.venue: Lyric Hyperion Theatre, 2106 Hyperion Ave, Silver Lake
events.blurb: 3 storytellers → improv team riffs off whatever they just heard.
events.description: Hybrid storytelling + improv show at the Lyric Hyperion. Three storytellers share a true, wild, or heartfelt moment; the improv team (co-hosted by Adam Macias and Dina Mild of Moscow Improv Club) launches into unscripted scenes inspired by what they just heard. Supportive, loose, joyfully chaotic.

events.title: Flavor Fest 2026 — with Tommy the Clown
events.blurb: All-day food + music fest. Free. Tommy the Clown performing.
events.description: One-day culture + food festival at Thunder Studios Long Beach: soul food, Caribbean, BBQ, sweets, juices, and more from curated SoCal vendors and food trucks. DJs and performers on the main stage with Tommy the Clown as a featured entertainer. Marketplace for art/fashion/cultural goods. Free entry.

events.title: Daft Disko LA — French House & Disco Party
events.blurb: ORLOVE presents: Daft Punk, Stardust, Breakbot, Justice, Cassius all night. 21+.
events.description: French-touch & disco-house dance party inspired by Daft Punk and peers (Stardust, Breakbot, Justice, Cassius, etc.). 21+, general admission standing room at the intimate Moroccan Lounge. Tickets from $22.

events.title: East Los Lucha: Lucha Xtrema
events.blurb: Local lucha libre: high-flying acrobatics, face-paint, rivalries. All ages.
events.description: East Los Lucha brings the best professional luchadors in the LA area to Don Quixote for a Sunday-evening card of high-flying maneuvers and character rivalries. Affordable, family-welcome. The homegrown Boyle Heights lucha scene at its most accessible.

events.title: 5th Annual AAPI LA Market × Smorgasburg
events.blurb: AAPI chefs, bakers, brands, and performers take over Smorgasburg for the day.
events.description: Fifth-annual AAPI LA Market in collaboration with Smorgasburg LA: AAPI chefs, bakers, cookbook authors, brands, and performers fill the five-acre Smorgasburg site at ROW DTLA for one day only. Live music, community activities, baked goods, savory bites, merch. Free.

events.title: Renaissance Pleasure Faire — FINAL weekend
events.venue: Santa Fe Dam Rec Area, 15501 E Arrow Hwy, Irwindale
events.blurb: Last weekend of the run. 20-acre Elizabethan world, armored jousts, costumes welcome.
events.description: Closing weekend of the 2026 run. Twenty acres of Elizabethan re-enactment in Irwindale: armored jousting tournaments, period stage acts, costumed vendors, tea with the queen. Costumes encouraged but not required. Tickets ~$53 adult.

events.title: Sunset Vibes Silent Disco at Venice Beach
events.blurb: 5 DJs on 5 channels, wireless headphones, beachfront.
events.description: Recurring silent disco at Venice's Waterfront: pick one of five DJ channels through wireless LED headphones, dance on the beach at sunset. Dining on-site. Runs weekly — check Fever for the current Saturday slot.

events.title: It's My Friday Every Sunday — Strange Hotels at Davey Wayne's
events.venue: Good Times at Davey Wayne's, 1611 N El Centro Ave, Hollywood
events.blurb: Weekly 70s-themed Sunday party inside a walk-through-refrigerator speakeasy.
events.description: Recurring Sunday-night party at the 70s-themed Hollywood speakeasy (entrance is through a vintage refrigerator in a fake living room). Indie-electro duo Strange Hotels performs live, DJ spins 70s deep cuts, charismatic host emceeing. Dress code: California casual, no athletic wear after dark.

events.title: Smorgasburg LA — AAPI Month Sunday
events.blurb: Regular Sunday Smorgasburg runs alongside the AAPI Market.
events.description: The standing Sunday Smorgasburg runs concurrently with the AAPI Market takeover (see w4-04). 5-acre food + design + vintage market, AAPI-spotlight continues.
